name: Open redirect — unvalidated redirect target
description: Requests a redirect endpoint with an attacker-controlled target URL (?url=https://evil.example) and checks the response. When the server issues a 3xx redirect whose Location is the caller-supplied external URL with no allow-list validation, an attacker can craft a link on this trusted origin that bounces the victim to an attacker-controlled site — a phishing amplifier and a classic OAuth/OIDC token-theft vector (a redirect_uri-style bounce that leaks tokens or codes in the URL). The predicate requires a redirect status AND the Location echoing the exact attacker origin, so an in-scope relative redirect doesn't trip it.

vulnerability.remediation: Never redirect to a caller-supplied absolute URL. Validate the target against a server-side allow-list of trusted hosts, or accept only relative paths (reject anything with a scheme or `//` authority). ASP.NET Core: use `Url.IsLocalUrl(target)` before `Redirect(target)` and fall back to a safe default otherwise. For OAuth/OIDC flows, match redirect_uri exactly against pre-registered values. Verify with `curl -k -sD - 'https://host/redirect?url=https://evil.example'` — a fixed server returns a same-origin Location (or 400), never `Location: https://evil.example`.
